name: Atak
classification_notes: Atak presents as a contemporary sans-serif with multiple weights and italics, consistent with a grotesque or neo-grotesque classification based on its naming conventions and foundry context.
summary: A six-weight grotesque sans from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, available in static and variable formats with matching italics across all weights.
description: Twelve styles span Light to Black with accompanying italics, covering a broad range of editorial and typographic applications. A variable font version accompanies the static cuts, offering additional flexibility across the weight axis. The foundry's wider catalogue suggests a design sensibility rooted in Swiss graphic culture and experimental type.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Atak as a 12-style family available in both static and variable formats, with weights ranging from Light to Black and italic companions throughout.

name: Copy
classification_notes: The weight range from Hair to Black with matching italics and a neutral, utility-focused naming convention points to a contemporary grotesque sans-serif. No stylistic ornamentation is suggested by the page content.
summary: A thirteen-style grotesque sans from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, spanning Hair to Black with italics across most weights.
description: Copy covers a wide tonal range from Hair to Black, with italic companions at Thin, Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights. The thirteen styles suggest a workhorse grotesque intended for varied typographic settings. Out of the Dark is a Swiss foundry with a catalogue that leans toward experimental and display work, making Copy a comparatively straight-faced offering.
foundry_blurb: Copy is listed in the Out of the Dark catalogue as a 13-style family, featuring weights from Hair through to Black with italic variants, positioned alongside the foundry's broader range of experimental and display typefaces.

name: Blitz
classification_notes: The page categorises Blitz as a Script, suggesting a display-oriented script typeface. Its single-style offering and the foundry's broader aesthetic of experimental and vernacular display types support a display script classification.
summary: A single-style display script from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, sitting within a catalogue of experimental and vernacular display types.
description: Out of the Dark offer Blitz as a one-style script, fitting into a catalogue that spans rough display faces, blackletter-adjacent designs and experimental vernacular typography. The single weight keeps it focused, suited to headlines, posters or logotype work where expressive lettering is the priority. Little metadata is exposed on the page, but the foundry's Swiss independent positioning and the surrounding typefaces suggest a raw, graphic sensibility.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Blitz as a Script typeface available in 1 style, part of a broader catalogue of experimental display and vernacular typefaces released by the Swiss independent foundry.

name: Crack
classification_notes: Crack is listed under the Headline category by the foundry, with a single style suggesting a purpose-built display typeface rather than a text or body family.
summary: A single-style headline display typeface from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, built for large-scale typographic impact.
description: Crack is a one-style display face from Out of the Dark, a Swiss foundry known for experimental and expressive type. Its placement in the foundry's Headline category suggests it is optimised for large settings rather than running text. With only a single style, it is a focused tool intended for editorial headlines or poster work.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Crack as a single-style headline typeface, categorised within their display-oriented Headline range alongside other expressive faces in their catalogue.

name: Cosplay
classification_notes: The name, sample text referencing chunky bones and razors, and single-style release all point to a display typeface with a distinctive, expressive character rather than a text or functional workhorse.
summary: A single-style display typeface from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, built around a distinctive expressive character.
description: Released as a single Regular style, Cosplay sits within Out of the Dark's catalogue of experimental and expressive display faces. The foundry's preview text references chunky, bone-like and razor-sharp formal qualities, suggesting a typeface with strong structural contrast or angular construction. Suited to editorial headlines, poster work and visual identities where a singular, committed voice is required.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Cosplay as a one-style display typeface, part of a broader catalogue of experimental type released from their Swiss studio.

name: Gaya
classification_notes: The page reveals a multi-weight sans-serif family with upright and italic cuts spanning Thin to Black, suggesting a functional grotesque or humanist sans; no further stylistic detail is available from the scraped content.
summary: A twelve-style sans-serif family from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, available in static and variable formats across six weights with matching italics.
description: Gaya spans six weights from Thin to Black, each paired with an italic, giving twelve static styles in total alongside a variable font version. The family sits within Out of the Dark's broader catalogue of display, experimental and text typefaces produced out of Switzerland. The combination of a full weight range and variable axis makes it suitable for both editorial and interface work.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Gaya as a 12-style family with variable font support, offered in both static and variable formats and previewed alongside their wider catalogue of experimental and functional typefaces.

name: Hammer
classification_notes: The two-style family consisting of a Bold and a Highlight variant, combined with the foundry's broader aesthetic of experimental and graphic typefaces, points to a display-oriented sans designed for impact at large sizes rather than extended text setting.
summary: A two-style display family from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, offering a Bold and a Highlight cut for large-scale graphic work.
description: With just two styles, Bold and Highlight, Hammer is built for typographic impact rather than versatility. The Highlight variant suggests a design intended for layering or decorative contrast against the primary weight. It sits within Out of the Dark's catalogue of experimental, graphically driven typefaces made for poster and display applications.
foundry_blurb: Hammer is offered in two styles: Hammer Bold and Hammer Highlight, as part of Out of the Dark's Swiss-based catalogue of experimental display typefaces.

name: Handwerk
classification_notes: The source material reference to Berthold Ludwig Wolpe and the single-weight offering suggest a display-oriented script or lettering revival, consistent with Wolpe's known work in calligraphic and inscriptional forms.
summary: A single-style display typeface from Out of the Dark, drawing on source material attributed to Berthold Ludwig Wolpe.
description: Released as a single Regular style, Handwerk takes its cues from the lettering work of Berthold Ludwig Wolpe, the German-British designer known for Albertus and his calligraphic inscriptional work. Out of the Dark have distilled this into one compact release with no weight range or italics, suited to headline and display use where handcrafted character is needed. The tight scope keeps it focused rather than versatile.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Handwerk as a single-style typeface with source material traced to Berthold Ludwig Wolpe, available in one Regular weight.

name: Gza
classification_notes: The presence of condensed and compressed widths alongside a 'Super' display cut, combined with the minimal preview text, points to a grotesque sans-serif with a functional, utilitarian character typical of Swiss type design.
summary: An eight-style grotesque from Out of the Dark offering weights from Regular to Bold, extended by condensed, compressed and super-display cuts.
description: Gza spans eight styles across three weights — Regular, Semibold and Bold — each with corresponding italics where applicable, and stretches into Condensed, Compressed and Super variants for tighter setting and large-scale work. The family's range of widths gives it flexibility across body text and headline use without requiring a separate display face. Released by the Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, it sits alongside similarly taut, economical typefaces in their catalogue.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Gza as an eight-style family, including Bold, Semibold, Semibold Italic, Regular, Regular Italic, Condensed, Compressed and Super cuts.

name: Play Extra
classification_notes: The source material reference to 1952 wooden poster characters (caractères en bois pour l'affiche) from Jacoby Fils in Grenoble places this firmly in the wood type display tradition, with a single bold style and variable font indicating a poster-oriented display typeface.
summary: A single-weight display typeface drawn from 1952 French wood type specimens by Jacoby Fils of Grenoble, available in static and variable formats.
description: Rooted in mid-century French wood poster type from the Jacoby Fils foundry in Grenoble, Play Extra arrives as a single bold style with a variable font counterpart. The source material dates to 1952, lending the design a raw, physical weight typical of large-scale wooden letterforms. One style, one purpose: large-scale setting where the blunt energy of wood type is the point.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Play Extra as a typeface drawn from wooden poster characters (caractères en bois pour l'affiche) from 1952, attributed to Jacoby Fils of Grenoble, available in both static and variable formats.

name: Plakat
classification_notes: The single weight is named Black and the typeface is called Plakat, suggesting a heavy display orientation typical of Swiss poster lettering; the limited style count and name point to a blackletter or heavy display category rooted in poster tradition.
summary: A single-weight Black display face from Out of the Dark, designed for heavy poster applications in the Swiss typographic tradition.
description: Available in one style only, Plakat is built around a single Black weight and carries the directness its name implies. Out of the Dark position it as a poster face, stripping back any multi-weight flexibility in favour of a committed, heavy presence on the page. The limited style count suggests a typeface made for impact at large sizes rather than extended text use.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark offer Plakat as a single-style release in Black, listed alongside their wider experimental catalogue of display and text typefaces.

name: Monoform
classification_notes: Monoform is a display typeface with monospaced geometric construction, available in solid, pattern, grid and colour variants suggesting a modular, experimental character suited to display use rather than text setting.
summary: A modular display typeface built on a monospaced grid, offered in solid, pattern and colour-layered variants across text and display optical sizes.
description: Monoform is structured around a geometric, monospaced grid and comes in ten styles split between standard and display cuts. The variants — Solid, Pattern, Grid, 2Color and 3Color — suggest a layering system designed for visual complexity, from clean single-colour setting to multi-colour compositions. It reads as a Swiss foundry's approach to modular type for poster and identity work.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark lists Monoform as a 10-style family with two size ranges — standard and Display — each offered in Solid, Pattern, Grid, 2Color and 3Color variants.

name: Quick
classification_notes: Quick comprises two expressive hand-tool styles — Marker and Brush — placing it firmly in the display script/lettering category rather than a conventional text typeface.
summary: A two-style display family from Out of the Dark offering a Marker and a Brush variant, each capturing a distinct hand-tool texture.
description: Quick pairs two hand-drawn display cuts — Marker and Brush — suited to work that calls for raw, gestural energy rather than refined letterforms. The two styles differ in tool feel, with the Brush variant carrying looser, more fluid strokes against the firmer, drier quality of the Marker. With only two styles the family is narrow in scope, but focused in intent.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Quick as a two-style typeface available in Marker and Brush variants, listed as 2 Styles in their catalogue.

name: Protokoll
classification_notes: The name and style range suggest a functional, structured grotesque sans; the weight range from Light to Bold with italics is characteristic of a workhorse grotesque intended for editorial or corporate use.
summary: A four-weight grotesque sans from Out of the Dark, offered in eight styles including matching italics across Light, Regular, Medium and Bold.
description: Protokoll covers a practical range of four weights from Light to Bold, each with a corresponding italic, giving eight styles in total. The naming and structure suggest a workhorse grotesque suited to editorial, corporate and publication contexts. It sits within a broad Swiss-inflected catalogue from Out of the Dark that spans experimental display faces and functional text families.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Protokoll as an 8-style family, comprising Light, Regular, Medium and Bold weights each paired with an italic. It is available as part of their typeface catalogue alongside trial versions.

name: Rauschen A
classification_notes: The family's structure, with a range of weights, italics, and a variable font, suggests a contemporary grotesque or humanist sans intended for versatile text and display use.
summary: A six-weight grotesque from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, available as static styles and a variable font across 12 styles including italics.
description: Rauschen A offers six weights from Regular to Black, each with a matching italic, totalling 12 static styles alongside a variable font version. It sits within Out of the Dark's broader Rauschen family, which includes Rauschen B and Rauschen Max as related releases. The source material cited on the page, a 2005 Blender magazine article, hints at a vernacular or subcultural design reference underpinning the family.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Rauschen A as part of a wider Rauschen family, offered in 12 styles plus a variable font, with source material drawn from a 2005 Blender magazine feature.

name: Raw
classification_notes: Raw presents as a single-weight display typeface with a bold, expressive character, consistent with the experimental display output of Out of the Dark's catalogue.
summary: A single-weight bold display typeface from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, built for headline and poster use.
description: Raw is a one-style display face offered in a single Bold weight, part of Out of the Dark's catalogue of experimental Swiss typefaces. The minimal style count suggests a focused, purpose-built design intended for large-scale or high-impact use rather than extended text setting. Sits alongside similarly expressive and unconventional releases from the same foundry.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Raw as a single-style typeface available in Bold, presented within a catalogue of experimental and unconventional display faces from their Swiss foundry.

name: Rauschen Max
classification_notes: The single-style release and the preview specimen suggesting a bold, expressive character point to a display-oriented sans, positioned within Out of the Dark's experimental typeface catalogue.
summary: A single-style display typeface from Out of the Dark, related to the broader Rauschen family released by the Swiss foundry.
description: Rauschen Max is a one-style release sitting alongside Rauschen A and Rauschen B in Out of the Dark's catalogue, suggesting it occupies an extreme or maximal position within that family's design logic. The foundry's work tends toward the experimental and typographically confrontational, and the preview specimen with its mix of currencies, punctuation and uppercase German text reinforces a display-first intent. With a single style on offer, it is a focused tool rather than a versatile system.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Rauschen Max as a single-style typeface, part of their Rauschen series alongside Rauschen A and Rauschen B, available from their Swiss type foundry.

name: Rauschen B
classification_notes: The family name and its companion Rauschen A suggest a systematic grotesque or neo-grotesque structure; the weight range and italic complement are consistent with a workhorse text or display sans-serif.
summary: A twelve-style grotesque family with variable font support, forming part of Out of the Dark's broader Rauschen series alongside Rauschen A and Rauschen Max.
description: Rauschen B offers six weights from Regular to Black, each with a matching italic, plus a variable font axis. It sits within a wider Rauschen series that includes Rauschen A and Rauschen Max, suggesting a structured system designed for range and consistency across different typographic contexts. The source material references electronic music history, hinting at a conceptual underpinning tied to sound and signal.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Rauschen B as a 12-style family with variable font support, listing Regular, Book, Medium, Semibold, Bold and Black weights alongside their italics. It is one of several releases in the Rauschen series from the Swiss foundry.

name: Remix A
classification_notes: The limited style count and Swiss foundry context suggest a stripped-back grotesque or display sans; the mono variant confirms a functional, neutral design lineage rather than an expressive or script-adjacent one.
summary: A compact three-style sans from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, offered in Regular, Italic and a monospaced variant.
description: Remix A is a small family of three styles covering Regular, Italic and Mono cuts, sitting within Out of the Dark's broader catalogue of experimental and functional Swiss typefaces. The inclusion of a monospaced variant alongside the standard cuts makes it usable across both editorial and screen or code contexts. The foundry's naming convention suggests a sibling relationship with Remix B, implying the two share formal DNA across separate releases.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Remix A as a three-style family comprising Regular, Italic and Mono, listed alongside its companion Remix B in the foundry's catalogue.

name: Resonanz A
classification_notes: The limited page content and the naming convention alongside sibling typefaces suggest a display or grotesque sans-serif orientation; the weight range from Regular to Black is consistent with a text or display grotesque family.
summary: A six-weight sans-serif from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, running from Regular through to Black with no italics.
description: Resonanz A is one half of a paired release alongside Resonanz B, both offering six weights from Regular to Black. The foundry draws on experimental and electronic source material as reference points, suggesting a design sensibility informed by signal, sound and technical imagery. With no italic or condensed cuts, it reads as a workhorse display grotesque built for weight-driven typographic contrasts.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Resonanz A as a six-style typeface family, referencing electronics and sound synthesis source material, including a 1973 article on sound synthesisers from Practical Electronics magazine.

name: Remix B
classification_notes: The page provides minimal detail, but the family structure with Regular, Italic and Mono styles, alongside the broader Out of the Dark catalogue aesthetic, points to a contemporary grotesque or neutral sans-serif design.
summary: A three-style sans-serif from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, offering Regular, Italic and a Mono variant within a single family.
description: Part of Out of the Dark's Remix series alongside Remix A, this family ships in three styles: Regular, Italic and Mono. The inclusion of a monospaced cut alongside the proportional styles suggests a design intended to cross between editorial and technical or screen-based contexts. The foundry's broader catalogue favours sharp, stripped-back design consistent with contemporary Swiss type production.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list Remix B as a three-style typeface family comprising Regular, Italic and Mono cuts, presented as part of their wider Swiss type catalogue.

name: SoLow
classification_notes: The name, the preview text, and the two-style offering (Regular and Italic) suggest a display-oriented typeface with a distinctive, expressive character rather than a workhorse text face.
summary: A two-style display typeface from Swiss independent foundry Out of the Dark, available in Regular and Italic.
description: SoLow is a compact release from Out of the Dark, offering just two styles: Regular and Italic. It sits within a catalogue of experimental and expressive typefaces from the Swiss foundry, suggesting a similarly unconventional character. The preview text alone conveys a casual, irreverent tone that points toward editorial and cultural use cases.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark list SoLow as a two-style family comprising a Regular and an Italic, offered alongside a preview, glyph set and source material section on the foundry's site.

name: Rza
classification_notes: The specimen and weight range suggest a contemporary grotesque sans-serif with a variable axis; no italics or condensed variants are listed, pointing to a single-width upright grotesque family.
summary: A six-weight grotesque sans from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, available in both static and variable formats.
description: Rza offers six weights from Light to Black in a clean, upright grotesque form, with a variable font option alongside the static styles. The foundry's catalogue leans heavily into experimental and display-oriented work, and Rza sits at the more restrained end of that range. With no italics or condensed cuts, it reads as a focused single-axis grotesque built for clarity and typographic economy.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Rza as a six-style grotesque family with a variable version, shown in both static and variable modes across the specimen.

name: Resonanz B
classification_notes: The limited style count, foundry context of experimental and display-oriented typefaces, and lack of italic suggest a display-focused sans with a distinctive character rather than a workhorse text family.
summary: A six-weight display sans from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, forming the B variant of the Resonanz family alongside Resonanz A.
description: Resonanz B is one half of a paired family, running from Regular through to Black across six weights with no italic complement. Out of the Dark situates the design within a broader catalogue of experimental and visually driven typefaces, drawing on references that include early 1970s electronics culture. The source material credit to a 1973 Practical Electronics article on sound synthesis suggests the design takes conceptual cues from analogue signal and waveform aesthetics.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present Resonanz B as a six-style family, one of two variants in the Resonanz series, with source material drawn from a 1973 Practical Electronics article on sound synthesisers.

name: ToY
classification_notes: The minimal page content and foundry context suggest a highly stylised, experimental display face; Out of the Dark specialises in expressive, unconventional type and ToY fits that pattern with a single style offering.
summary: A single-style experimental display face from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, released as a standalone cut with no weight or width variants.
description: Out of the Dark position ToY as a self-contained display typeface with just one style, keeping it focused and deliberately limited in scope. The foundry, based in Switzerland, is known for expressive and unconventional type design, and ToY sits within that catalogue of experimental releases. Its reference material draws from archival printed matter, suggesting a retro or vernacular source influence.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present ToY as a single-style typeface available in Regular, listed alongside their broader catalogue of experimental and display-oriented releases.

name: SubSans
classification_notes: Named SubSans and presented as a sans-serif family with a broad weight range; the naming convention and style range suggest a straightforward grotesque or neutral sans classification.
summary: A fourteen-style sans-serif from Out of the Dark covering seven weights from Hairline to Black, each with a matching italic.
description: SubSans spans an unusually wide weight range, from Hairline at one extreme to Black at the other, with six intermediate cuts and italic companions throughout. The family offers solid coverage for editorial and identity work where consistent weight progression matters. Out of the Dark position it alongside a catalogue of experimental and display-oriented releases, making SubSans a more restrained, functional offering in their library.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark present SubSans as a 14-style family running from Hairline and Hairline Italic through Thin, Light, Regular, Medium, Bold and Black, each with a corresponding italic.

name: Syncro
classification_notes: The family includes upright and italic cuts alongside a monospaced variant, suggesting a functional grotesque with utilitarian intentions. The naming conventions and Swiss foundry context point to a structured, neutral grotesque lineage.
summary: A five-style grotesque from Swiss foundry Out of the Dark, spanning Book and Bold weights with italic and a monospaced companion.
description: Syncro offers a compact five-style family covering Book and Bold uprights, an italic, and two mono cuts including a Mono Italic. The inclusion of a monospaced variant suggests applications across editorial, UI and coded or technical contexts. Released by Out of the Dark, a Swiss foundry known for tightly scoped, purposeful type releases.
foundry_blurb: Out of the Dark lists Syncro as a five-style family comprising Bold, Book, Italic, Mono and Mono Italic cuts, shown alongside source material referencing Franz Kafka's Die Verwandlung, published in Austria-Hungary in 1915.
